120784.explanation: A referendum is a means to gather the opinions of residents. There are many ways to collect residents’ opinions. Involving residents prior to decision-making is very important to PGB; a referendum is one means to do this, but there can also be other forms.

120787.explanation: "Always" is very definite. The opening of a shop or campsite is considered on a case-by-case basis, as we do for all initiators. The possibility to sell small-scale own products and/or regional products must remain possible.

120788.explanation: PGB believes that financial support should be arranged by the national government to ensure a level playing field. The municipality can provide support through knowledge and expertise.

120789.explanation: Distribution centers often generate insufficient employment for the municipality’s residents. However, they do lead to extra traffic, which PGB finds undesirable.

120790.explanation: The extension of the asylum seekers’ center (AZC) and the agreements made have brought peace. The nuisance has significantly decreased. Thus, the municipality has determined for a long period that predominantly status holders are placed in the Oisterwijk AZC.

120791.explanation: PGB believes that agriculture and horticulture should not have to arrange this themselves while other sectors do not. Housing for seasonal workers is something the municipality must arrange together with employers. The municipality must ensure that the housing is adequate.

120793.explanation: PGB wants to reduce CO2 emissions, including those from open fireplaces and wood stoves.

120794.explanation: Terraces can also be heated with sustainable energy, which means no CO2 emissions are produced.

120795.explanation: From the moment a nationwide fireworks ban is in effect.

120800.explanation: In Oisterwijk, we already do this with participation schemes. These apply not only to people on welfare benefits but also to those with slightly higher incomes (up to 120% of the welfare benefit level).

120801.explanation: The local broadcaster is important for the identity of our communities. Now that local broadcasters are required to cooperate and merge into regional broadcasters, it is important that events significant to our residents remain visible on TV.

120803.explanation: PGB believes every primary school child should have the opportunity to develop through sports and/or culture. This is not limited to music lessons. The municipality should provide subsidies for children who cannot afford it.

120807.explanation: PGB wants continued focus on local crime fighting and a police force that is easily accessible and recognizable. Currently, residents feel safe, partly due to the presence of enforcement officers (BOAs). Therefore, more enforcers are not needed.

120808.explanation: PGB is not in favor of more camera surveillance without specific reasons. Where there is a lot of nuisance and cameras can help, PGB believes it is appropriate to discuss with residents what possibilities exist and for how long.

120809.explanation: These are facilities already arranged regionally.

120811.explanation: PGB continues to seek ways to provide priority to our own residents in the allocation of social rental housing.

120813.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for recreational stays, not permanent residence. But if parks are no longer attractive to tourists and cannot be renovated, permanent residence may be allowed. Especially if the parks would otherwise fall into disrepair.

120814.explanation: Outside the existing village boundaries, yes, but not just anywhere within the municipality. The distinction between built-up areas and nature/agricultural sectors must remain.

120815.explanation: PGB focuses on prevention in the social domain. It believes that benefits are essentially temporary support between two jobs. If no work is available, a person can gain work experience and stay involved in society through socially meaningful work.

120817.explanation: In Oisterwijk, the threshold is 120%, as in many surrounding municipalities. Any chosen threshold will lead to discussion. Therefore, PGB opts to maintain the current threshold. For residents above this income limit, other forms of support are available within our municipality.

120819.explanation: Installing more charging stations is fine, but the parking lot must also be usable by people without electric cars. Otherwise, the parking problem will only worsen.

120820.explanation: For most of the built-up area, the speed limit may be reduced and more attention can be given to slow traffic. Only on through roads can 50 km/h be maintained.

120821.explanation: Residents must receive the care they need. This is not dependent on their religion, culture, or beliefs. Within the possibilities available, this can be taken into account.

120826.explanation: PGB believes that current sites should be reviewed first. Are they being well used now? If additional space is still needed, then it can be examined whether such space is available.

120829.explanation: Waste processing costs money. The fact that disposing of waste at the recycling center is free does not mean waste processing is free. In that case, everyone pays via taxes regardless of how much waste they produce. PGB believes this is not a fair distribution.

120830.explanation: PGB wants more solar panels and to reduce CO2 emissions. To make this possible, allowing solar panels on agricultural land is an option.

120832.explanation: PGB even aims for 35%. This includes social and affordable owner-occupied homes.

120835.explanation: PGB is not in favor of more parking spaces in the centers. Where there are parking spaces, there cannot be a home, shop, or event. Only if more homes or shops/businesses are built should the number of parking spaces increase.

120837.explanation: Haaren needs a new community center. This will bring activity to the center of Haaren and is good for residents' well-being.

120838.explanation: Due to accessibility for the less able, PGB believes completely closing off is not a good idea. It is certainly possible to look with entrepreneurs and residents at ways to make De Lind even more attractive.

120784.explanation: Democratic decision-making with democratically elected representatives suffices.

120787.explanation: We want to give appropriate side activities a chance, but they must not conflict with the livability of the centers and immediate surroundings.

120788.explanation: Sustainability is a top priority for us in the coming years. We encourage it but do not see its financing as a core municipal task.

120789.explanation: Strongly dependent on size and added value for Oisterwijk.

120790.explanation: We actively encourage asylum seekers at the AZC, who are allowed, to work and contribute to our local society.

120791.explanation: "Must" is too strong a word. As a principle, yes, but with humane housing that prevents nuisance and exploitation.

120793.explanation: Provided it does not cause disproportionate nuisance.

120794.explanation: This facility partly generates extra economic activities and income for entrepreneurs. We do advise entrepreneurs to choose sustainable solutions.

120795.explanation: A great idea to compensate for the national ban.

120801.explanation: We attach great importance to local information provision itself, especially in light of all the national and regional developments in broadcasting. We closely monitor these.

120803.explanation: Yes, we are already doing this. VVD places great value on the development of every child.

120807.explanation: Safety is an important election theme for the VVD. We must continue to anticipate this by investing.

120808.explanation: We find prevention and increasing the chance of apprehension important.

120809.explanation: We reject violence against women. The location for shelter does not necessarily have to be in Oisterwijk but should be within a reasonable distance.

120811.explanation: It is difficult for starters to find a home in their own municipality.

120813.explanation: For some parks without a tourism perspective, we apply tailor-made solutions.

120814.explanation: Tailor-made measures apply here per situation as well. Spacious green buffers with neighboring municipalities are especially desirable. Municipalities should not grow into one another.

120815.explanation: It offers opportunities to advance to paid work and provides a counter-performance because rights and duties go hand in hand.

120817.explanation: Oisterwijk has long applied a generous policy in this regard.

120820.explanation: Speed must be appropriate to the function of the road. Safety comes first.

120821.explanation: Completely contradicts our five liberal core values: freedom, responsibility, tolerance, social justice, and equality.

120826.explanation: Positive economic developments align with our ideas. We seek a combination of revitalizing current sites with expansion in hectares. Entrepreneurs are the engine of the local economy and prosperity.

120830.explanation: Provided it fits within spatial policy and regulations.

120832.explanation: The percentage is not definitive. Building according to need will increasingly become common practice. The percentage of social housing can be lower but can also be higher.

120835.explanation: Not only for cars but also for bicycles. We prefer underground solutions.

120837.explanation: We fully support the decisions made so far. We look forward to the operational plans the council has promised us.

120838.explanation: A bad plan in every respect. We want to keep De Lind accessible to all traffic users.

120784.explanation: PRO wants to involve residents early and let them participate in decision-making. A referendum can be a good tool but not always. For each topic, it must always be weighed whether a referendum is the best option.

120787.explanation: PRO makes a balancing of interests between the economy and nature; space can only be used once. Each case must be weighed based on what is possible. Nature is preferred over (intensive) agriculture.

120788.explanation: PRO stimulates sustainability and local energy initiatives.

120789.explanation: PRO would rather allocate this space to housing and nature than to the establishment of distribution centers.

120790.explanation: PRO stands for solidarity and the sheltering of vulnerable groups. PRO is therefore a strong supporter of keeping the AZC open and optimally facilitating it.

120791.explanation: Living and working are separate. It is undesirable for the employer also to be the landlord, as this creates an undesirable power imbalance. We want more social rental housing in Oisterwijk so that labor migrants can also live in a proper home.

120793.explanation: In the long term, PRO believes wood stoves should no longer be used for heating homes. At the moment, however, this can still have a cost-reducing effect and be necessary for residents with low incomes.

120794.explanation: Banning patio heaters on public terraces contributes to energy savings. In winter, people do not need to sit outside on a terrace but can better sit inside.

120795.explanation: New Year's Eve can also be celebrated with an alternative, such as a laser show. The initiative for this does not lie with the municipality.

120800.explanation: PRO promotes sports and health, also for people with low incomes.

120801.explanation: There is no reason to expand the current subsidy scheme.

120803.explanation: Music lessons should be accessible to everyone. If parents cannot afford it themselves, the municipality should pay for music lessons. Wealthier parents can pay for it themselves.

120807.explanation: Oisterwijk is not unsafe; there is no need for extra focus on safety. We do see the need for a green community officer who monitors nature and greenery.

120808.explanation: There is no need to expand camera surveillance. Moreover, it infringes on the privacy of our residents.

120809.explanation: This facility exists in cooperation with other municipalities in Hart-van-Brabant. Oisterwijk must ensure a safe shelter for Oisterwijk residents, which could even be better located elsewhere in the region than within the municipality itself.

120811.explanation: PRO wants fair housing for everyone. We collaborate regionally for social rental housing. Because Oisterwijk has relatively few social rental homes, withdrawing from regional cooperation would mean fewer chances on the housing market for Oisterwijk housing seekers.

120813.explanation: Many recreational homes in the municipality of Oisterwijk are very close to vulnerable nature (N2000). Allowing permanent residence in those areas puts more pressure on that nature. The Noenes in Haaren is an exception due to its nature and history as a hamlet.

120814.explanation: There is a significant housing construction task. To meet this, we must build outside the current boundaries. However, we are critical about where this is appropriate and believe infill development should occur first and only then expansion.

120815.explanation: Socially meaningful work enables people to participate again in society. This must be done based on trust in the resident and must be tailor-made, so it cannot simply be made mandatory.

120817.explanation: PRO has been advocating for years to raise this income limit from 120% to 130% of the minimum.

120819.explanation: This is part of sustainability and the energy transition. The extent to which current charging stations are used is an indication of the need to expand the number of charging stations.

120820.explanation: PRO has been striving for years to give priority to cyclists and pedestrians over fast traffic. This increases safety for slow traffic. Therefore, it is now explicitly included in the election program that the speed limit within all villages must be reduced to 30 km per hour.

120821.explanation: PRO stands for inclusive care and tailored solutions.

120826.explanation: PRO actually wants less growth space for companies because this comes at the expense of space for, for example, homes and nature.

120829.explanation: All residents are responsible for their own waste and must therefore pay for its removal and processing. Furthermore, reuse of items must be stimulated.

120830.explanation: PRO promotes sustainable energy, but we prefer solar panels on roofs rather than in pastures.

120832.explanation: Oisterwijk currently has a low percentage of social housing. Therefore, PRO wants at least 40% of new homes to be social housing. In the long term, PRO wants at least 25% of the total housing stock to be social housing.

120835.explanation: PRO actually wants less car traffic. Car use should be leading, not car ownership. Car sharing ensures that everyone remains mobile but fewer cars and thus fewer parking spaces are needed.

120837.explanation: Every village needs a good community center. However, we must carefully monitor costs, both for the building and later for operation.

120838.explanation: PRO has included this in the election program. Exceptions must be possible, for example, a few parking spaces for disabled people will need to be available.

120784.explanation: We support advisory referenda on topics that have a significant impact on residents.

120787.explanation: This is important for the future of farmers but must fit within the framework of nature and the environment.

120788.explanation: We believe sustainability is important and want to encourage residents rather than impose it.

120789.explanation: Currently, we have no distribution centers, and we believe that Brabant has enough better locations for distribution centers.

120790.explanation: We support the sheltering of people from war zones, but we want to exclude or send back as many fortune seekers as possible. Every six months, we will closely monitor the situation regarding nuisance. The nuisance must be reduced to zero. Otherwise, closure will be the only option.

120791.explanation: We have many good examples in our villages that show this works.

120793.explanation: We do not want to prohibit everything. Residents should be able to make their own considerations.

120794.explanation: We do not want to be a government that only prohibits things for entrepreneurs and residents.

120795.explanation: Our ideal scenario is that, in cooperation with local associations, a fireworks show is organized.

120800.explanation: From national policy, there are many initiatives, and at the municipal level, there are sufficient funds to prevent people from falling through the cracks.

120801.explanation: They are currently sufficiently supported from the municipal budget.

120803.explanation: This is already well arranged in our municipality.

120807.explanation: With more manpower, you can organize and guarantee more safety rather than just issuing fines.

120808.explanation: For some areas (unsafe ones), camera surveillance would be desirable.

120809.explanation: Violence against women can only be solved effectively by offering low-threshold assistance.

120811.explanation: CDA has arranged this initiative in the municipality.

120813.explanation: This must fit the character of the recreational park. So, not at campsites.

120814.explanation: Only if this fits within the zoning plans. Adding a street along the edge of the village boundary should be possible, as well as space-for-space housing.

120815.explanation: Every resident must contribute to a healthy society.

120817.explanation: We consider the standard of 120% sufficient. However, we want better enforcement and also better consideration of the deployment of aid services.

120819.explanation: As long as the energy supply is not in order, this is not realistic.

120820.explanation: It must be adaptable within the respective areas.

120821.explanation: No role for the municipality but for the health insurers.

120826.explanation: Expansion only based on the needs of local entrepreneurs. CDA also finds it important to intensify existing industrial estates.

120829.explanation: For all recyclable waste yes, but not for non-recyclable waste.

120830.explanation: Place solar panels first on buildings and along highways.

120832.explanation: We want at least 30% social rental housing and/or affordable owner-occupied homes.

120835.explanation: We aim for parking spaces with grass pavers to benefit nature and drainage.

120837.explanation: The current community center is extremely outdated, and we now have a nice location in the center that is being developed.

120838.explanation: We want to keep the center accessible for pedestrians, cyclists, and cars.

120784.explanation: D66 believes residents must be seriously involved in major and impactful decisions. What we do not want: referenda as blunt yes/no instruments. Yes to participation and all forms of engagement where residents can have their say. No to pseudo participation.

120787.explanation: D66 supports broadening farm businesses, such as shops, care functions, or small-scale campsites. But not always and everywhere. The municipality must continue to weigh what fits with nature, the environment, traffic, and livability.

120788.explanation: D66 supports residents who jointly generate sustainable energy. Local initiatives create support, accelerate the energy transition, and keep the benefits within the local municipality. The municipality can provide financial support and ensures clear rules.

120789.explanation: No distribution boxes at the village entrance, no companies that mainly demand space but provide few jobs. We are open to new businesses and do make room for companies that are locally anchored.

120790.explanation: D66 stands for humanity and responsibility. Asylum reception is a collective task that should not be avoided by quickly closing down. We stand for good, humane reception in Oisterwijk, with attention to support, livability, and proper guidance.

120791.explanation: D66 believes employers should take responsibility for decent housing for seasonal workers, preferably on or near the company. Provided it is well integrated, safe, and mindful of the surroundings. This prevents nuisance and exploitation.

120793.explanation: D66 Oisterwijk chooses a sensible and fair approach. No ban from one day to the next, but steps that work and have support. With poor air quality, for example during a burning alert, we find it logical that wood burning is temporarily discouraged.

120794.explanation: D66 wants to counteract energy waste. Terrace heaters using gas or electricity do not fit with a sustainable municipality. Heating outside while saving inside is no longer appropriate.

120795.explanation: D66 supports a municipality-organized New Year's Eve show, with lasers or drones. This is safer, clearer, and more enjoyable than private fireworks everywhere. This way we celebrate New Year's together, with less nuisance and more fun.

120800.explanation: Poverty is not a personal fault. People dependent on social assistance deserve support, just like the growing group of working poor who often remain invisible. No one should have to choose between food, healthcare, sports, or energy.

120801.explanation: Local journalism is indispensable for a healthy democracy. That is why we support a strong, independent local broadcaster. Democracy only works if residents know what is happening.

120803.explanation: D66 believes every primary school child should have access to music education, if the child wishes. Music stimulates creativity and self-confidence. Municipal subsidies ensure this does not depend on parents’ income.

120807.explanation: D66 believes sufficient enforcement officers are important for safety and livability. For visibility, prevention, and intervention where necessary.

120808.explanation: D66 believes camera surveillance is a serious measure affecting privacy. It may only be used if necessary and proportionate and if other measures are insufficient. So as a last resort and targeted for specific security problems.

120809.explanation: There is a great shortage in the Netherlands of sufficient safe shelters for victims of domestic violence. D66 believes victims must always be able to count on a safe place. That is more important than location. Regional cooperation can sometimes provide better or faster help.

120811.explanation: Social rental homes must be assigned fairly because housing shortages do not stop at municipal borders. No priority based on origin or place of residence. Everyone deserves equal chances for affordable housing. We advocate a regional approach.

120813.explanation: D66 wants to allow permanent residence in specific parks. Instead of a problem, we see this as a solution. Permanent residence has been tolerated on several parks for over 30 years. On these parks, the chalets are privately owned.

120814.explanation: We will not solve the housing shortage by only densifying within municipal borders. Building outside municipal borders is therefore inevitable. However, it must fit within the rural and green character of the outer edges of our villages.

120815.explanation: D66 opposes mandatory reciprocal obligations. People with benefits are helped forward with trust, guidance, and suitable work—not with coercion. Voluntary contribution is of course always possible.

120817.explanation: D66 supports raising the income limit from 120% to 130%. The difference may seem small, but the effect is big. It is the difference between surviving and participating. It is a way to give more people access to support, such as energy allowance and other municipal schemes.

120819.explanation: We install additional charging stations based on demonstrable need, available grid capacity, and available space. No uncontrolled growth. This makes clean driving more accessible and accelerates the switch to sustainable transport. Practical solutions like cable ducts should also be considered.

120820.explanation: D66 supports a 30 km/h speed limit within built-up areas: it is safer, quieter, healthier, and more logical. Most streets in our centres are living streets, not racing streets.

120821.explanation: Within limits of availability, professionalism, and reasonableness, D66 believes people should be able to choose care that fits their personal beliefs.

120826.explanation: Space is scarce and therefore valuable. That is why we want to use business parks more efficiently. First revitalize, green, and arrange smarter. Only then build new. Spatial quality is central, above expanding hectares.

120829.explanation: D66 believes that disposing of waste at the recycling center does not need to be completely free. A small contribution encourages reuse and less waste. However, we ensure it remains affordable for residents and that everyone can properly dispose of their waste.

120830.explanation: D66 does not want to ban it outright but believes it can only happen under conditions, with support, smart integration, and first considering better alternatives like roofs and industrial estates.

120832.explanation: D66 wants at least 35% of total housing in the municipality to be social housing. Not as a rigid norm per project, but as a municipality-wide target we work towards together in all plans.

120835.explanation: Parking must fit our villages. We critically review parking standards and create spaces at the edges of the centers where possible. This way, parking is not only functional but also an opportunity to make our villages greener and more pleasant.

120837.explanation: D66 does not believe the municipality should abandon building a new community center. A good village hall is the heart of the village: for social gatherings, associations, and caring for each other. Haaren deserves a beautiful, future-proof village house.

120838.explanation: We are not against cars but for people. An accessible and pleasant center requires calm, space, and at the same time supports the local economy. That is why we are exploring a car-reduced design of the village center in Oisterwijk with more space for pedestrians, cyclists, and greenery.

120788.explanation: The municipality cannot do this alone; support from the province or national government is necessary.

120789.explanation: Our municipality is too small for this.

120790.explanation: Asylum accommodation is intended for people who really need it.

120791.explanation: This is not desirable at all locations.

120793.explanation: This is a difficult issue; we do not want nuisance for people with lung problems. But we also do not want to forbid people who have been burning wood for years and enjoy sitting outside, nor impose costs on them.

120794.explanation: We want vibrant centers where people can enjoy sitting outside for a long time.

120795.explanation: A beautiful connection where we can all enjoy the fireworks together.

120800.explanation: People with social assistance should be helped but can also contribute.

120801.explanation: We see the added value of our local broadcasters.

120803.explanation: Every child should be able to make their own choice.

120807.explanation: We would like to see a more positive attitude from our enforcement officers. So that people cooperate and fewer fines are issued. Preferably in a problem-solving atmosphere, with an open, friendly, and helpful attitude.

120809.explanation: If necessary, it must be implemented.

120811.explanation: Too many local residents have to look for housing outside their own municipality. People have connections here, often grew up here, and contribute to a strong village.

120813.explanation: Recreational homes are meant for recreation.

120814.explanation: We need space to be able to build more. Hardening of the core is not desirable. On the edges we can also build cheaper, provided that greenery is included in the plans.

120815.explanation: It is good to participate in society. Everyone contributes their part. And it also helps you to get among people (for those to whom this applies).

120817.explanation: We are not talking about financial aid here, but real help truly helps people.

120820.explanation: In many places it is already 30 km per hour; on all roads this is not realistic.

120821.explanation: Everyone should be able to help each other but must have respect for each other's faith, culture, or belief.

120826.explanation: There is more need for business space.

120830.explanation: This is intended for food production.

120832.explanation: There are multiple solutions, for example splitting homes, vacant buildings/office spaces, etc.

120835.explanation: We do not want hardening in the cores, but parking garages can be built in places where construction is still planned, provided it is financially responsible.

120837.explanation: The residents deserve a beautiful and pleasant community center.

120784.explanation: The direct voice of the resident on a specific subject is very valuable and ultimately counts above all.

120787.explanation: It contributes to sustainable management of the agricultural business and gives residents the opportunity to consume locally.

120788.explanation: It is not the primary task of the municipality to facilitate climate policy.

120789.explanation: Distribution centers belong in or near large cities, not in a village environment.

120790.explanation: The number of shelter places must be reduced or at most remain the same. Closing them as quickly as possible is not feasible. It requires a careful, humane approach, because the refugee is also a victim of current policy.

120791.explanation: It is not the primary task of the municipality to organize housing for labor migrants. They or their employer must do this themselves, whether or not on their own premises.

120793.explanation: There is nothing wrong with lighting a fire. It also has a bonding effect on people.

120794.explanation: It is up to the hospitality entrepreneur whether or not to offer terrace heating.

120795.explanation: In Oisterwijk, individual fireworks are still part of the social cohesion in the neighborhood and, for now, part of our culture. When this phases out organically, not by banning it, a municipal show can be a good alternative.

120800.explanation: Membership of a sports club is not a basic necessity. Social assistance is intended to support people in their basic needs.

120803.explanation: It is the parents' responsibility to provide this.

120807.explanation: The current situation is adequate. In practice, enforcement officers are more often occupied with writing parking fines than with maintaining safety.

120808.explanation: More camera surveillance comes at the expense of our privacy.

120809.explanation: It is an official task of central municipalities, which Oisterwijk is not. There is also no need for it in Oisterwijk.

120811.explanation: Social rental housing should primarily be intended for local residents in this time of housing shortage.

120813.explanation: A home is a home. In this time of housing shortage, it is also only logical to use every possibility.

120814.explanation: Given the rural character of Oisterwijk, inner-city redevelopment has priority and preference.

120815.explanation: This increases opportunities in the labor market and ensures a healthier balance of giving and receiving.

120817.explanation: This stimulates participation in the labor market. Moreover, poverty should be solved at the root cause, so that fewer and fewer residents have to live in poverty.

120819.explanation: The facilities are good as they are.

120820.explanation: As long as the necessity has not been demonstrated, there is no reason to hinder traffic flow.

120821.explanation: In principle, care should be neutral regarding religious belief or culture. Nevertheless, in case of a strong mismatch in mutual connection, there must be room for another choice. This also applies in other situations (not related to religion).

120826.explanation: Companies provide education and employment. This contributes to our local prosperity and well-being.

120829.explanation: In principle, no. However, it might be interesting to investigate by how much the general waste disposal tax should increase to cover the costs of 'free' dumping, so that all waste processing in the municipality is jointly paid for.

120830.explanation: Solar panels are the asbestos of the future and are only attractive because of subsidies.

120832.explanation: This requirement disrupts customization, makes projects financially unfeasible, and thus hinders new housing development.

120835.explanation: For every new (housing) development in the core, we must at least apply the parking standard. And if other logical places for (free!) parking arise, we should seize those opportunities.

120837.explanation: The investment is disproportionate. Moreover, future operation will be problematic. Nevertheless, it is important that Den Domp and its activities receive attention in another appropriate way.

120838.explanation: It has been shown to be bad for the local economy and contradicts the original commercial character of Oisterwijk.

statements.title: The municipality should close De Lind in Oisterwijk to motor vehicle traffic.
The residents of Oisterwijk should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Oisterwijk moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted in Oisterwijk.

titleParts.text: In Oisterwijk moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Oisterwijk should have a women's shelter for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Oisterwijk.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Oisterwijk should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: At least 30% of the newly built homes in Oisterwijk should be social rental homes. In the Netherlands 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Oisterwijk moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Business parks in Oisterwijk may be allowed to expand.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: Villages and small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality must ban outdoor patio heaters.
The municipality should abandon plans to build a new community centre in Haaren.
